Exit Load: 0%

Risk: Very High

Inception Date: 1 January, 2013

About (Fund): The fund allocates at least 80% of its assets to equity, preference shares and fully convertible debentures. The remaining is invested in money market instruments to maintain liquidity. The fund enjoys

g is invested in money market instruments to maintain liquidity. The fund enjoys tax benefits under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act. Investors can reduce their tax liability by up to ₹46,800. Investments in the SBI long-term fund are spread across large, small and mid-cap stocks to ensure the portfolio is well diversified. The fund has a mandatory lock-in period of 3 years. Investment Strategy The fund is actively managed by Dinesh Balchandran,

3 years. Investment Strategy The fund is actively managed by Dinesh Balchandran, who follows a bottom-up stock-picking approach along with fundamental analysis. The investment process involves identifying companies with strong business models and sound management. Each stock in the portfolio is selected after a deep evaluation of its financial health. Who Should Invest In SBI Long Term Equity Fund - Direct Growth?You should invest in the SBI long

t In SBI Long Term Equity Fund - Direct Growth?You should invest in the SBI long-term fund if:-You are a first-time investor-You are looking for tax benefits-You want a diversified portfolio Taxation on SBI Long Term Fund Since equity-linked saving schemes have a lock-in period of 3 years, they are not subject to short-term capital gain (STCG). Any gains from selling the SBI long-term fund after 3 years are subject to long-term capital gain or LT

the SBI long-term fund after 3 years are subject to long-term capital gain or LTCG at the rate of 12.5%. Allocation & Historical Returns

Fund Manager: Milind Agrawal

How Do I Invest: 1. Download the INDmoney app. 2. Search for the fund. 3. Choose SIP or lump sum. 4. Enter the investment amount. 5. Complete payment using bank mandate or UPI.

Risk: Very High

AUM: ₹ 11.8K Cr

Inception Date: 1 January, 2013

TurnOver: 12.65%

About (Fund): that invests in all the companies that make up the Nifty 50 Index. This index represents India’s top 50 companies based on market capitalization. The fund allocates at least 95% of its total assets to the

arket capitalization. The fund allocates at least 95% of its total assets to these companies in the same proportion as the index, while the remaining 5% is invested in cash and money market instruments for liquidity purposes. The fund tracks the Nifty 50 TRIas its benchmark. This index reflects the overall performance of the 50 companies, including any dividends paid out. By mirroring this index, the fund aims to deliver returns that are closely

out. By mirroring this index, the fund aims to deliver returns that are closely aligned with the broader market performance. The fund managers do not try to outperform the index but rather aim to provide investors with a simple and efficient way to invest in India’s leading companies. Whenever there is a change in the composition of the index, the fund also rebalances its portfolio accordingly. Being a passively managed fund, it also benefits fro

its portfolio accordingly. Being a passively managed fund, it also benefits from a lower expense ratio, making it a cost-effective option for long-term investors.

Fund Manager: Pradeep Kesavan

How Do I Invest: 1. Download the INDmoney app. 2. Search for the fund. 3. Choose SIP or lump sum. 4. Enter the investment amount. 5. Complete payment using bank mandate or UPI.
